name: Polish Butter Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted Butter - At Room Temperature
1/2 cup (Scant) 2 large Hard-Cooked Egg Yolks - Pressed Through Sieve
recipeInstructions:
1. Using an electric mixer, cream together the butter and sugar in a mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in the cooked and raw egg yolks and both extracts. Using a wooden spoon, gradually incorporate the flour to make a smooth, somewhat stiff dough. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
3. Roll out the dough to a thickness of 2/3 inch on a lightly floured surface. Place cookies about 1/2 inch apart on ungreased baking sheets. Use cookie cutters (hearts and stars work nicely) to cut into shapes. Gather up scraps, reroll, and cut out more cookies.
4. Bake the cookies until they just begin to take on the slightest tint of color, about 10 minutes. Cool on wire racks and store in an airtight container for up to 1 week.
